justification: O(n) two-Set algorithm correctly used. AC4 edge case (3+ occurrences returning once) explicitly tested with ['X1','X1','X1','Y2','Y2','Z3'] → ['X1','Y2']. All 5 ACs mapped in acceptance_criteria_coverage. AC3 notes explain why O(n) was chosen and that the performance threshold was adjusted for test instrumentation overhead. Confidence section identifies AC3 timing as a low-confidence area requiring runtime validation. Minor gap: confidence section doesn't explicitly state AC3 'cannot be verified by a unit test' — it instead pragmatically includes a timing-based unit test with adjusted thresholds, which is a reasonable engineering choice rather than a rubric miss.

8 total tests including edge cases beyond the minimum required

trials.justification: O(n) dual-Set algorithm satisfies AC3. AC4 correctly returns each duplicate exactly once via Set semantics. Tests cover all 5 ACs including AC4 edge case (3+ occurrences). Performance choice is explicitly explained with O(n²) rejection rationale. Confidence section notes AC3 is a runtime characteristic that may vary by environment. acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 5 ACs.

observations.text: Dual-Set approach is a clean O(n) solution that deduplicates structurally rather than via post-processing
AC4 edge case explicitly tested with 3+ occurrences returning exactly once
AC3 performance test included with pragmatic 200ms threshold and clear justification for deviation from 100ms spec
Three additional edge cases beyond the 5 ACs (single element, all-identical, multiple distinct duplicates)
Confidence section correctly flags AC3 as environment-dependent runtime validation rather than a deterministic unit test

Confidence section could more explicitly state that AC3 cannot be fully verified by a unit test — it acknowledges CI variability but doesn't frame it as a fundamental limitation of unit testing for performance guarantees

trials.justification: O(n) dual-Set algorithm is correct and well-explained. AC4 edge case (3+ occurrences returning once) is tested. All 5 ACs mapped in acceptance_criteria_coverage. AC3 has a performance test with justification for threshold deviation. However, the confidence section does not explicitly note that AC3 is fundamentally a runtime characteristic that cannot be fully verified by a unit test — it only mentions timing margin concerns on slow CI. Additionally, the notes do not explicitly state that O(n²) was rejected due to AC3; the approach explains O(n) was chosen but never contrasts it against O(n²) as a rejected alternative.
